message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="All 5 polish tasks complete, CI green">
ALL POLISH TASKS COMPLETE! Every task is committed, pushed, and CI green.

Final status:
- Task #1: a11y error elements -- DONE (9cdc020)
- Task #2: Forgot password link -- DONE (6fc51ca)
- Task #3: HTMX progressive enhancement -- DONE (a68f9a2)
- Task #4: aria-live announcements -- DONE (554c928)
- Task #5: Password visibility toggle -- DONE (649fb5d)

The todo list app now has:
- All MVP features (US-1 through US-8) working
- HTMX progressive enhancement (works without JS too)
- Proper a11y: always-in-DOM errors, aria-live announcements, password toggle
- Forgot password placeholder page
- All tests passing, CI green

Great work, team. I'm going to report to the project owner and begin shutdown. Stand by for shutdown requests.
</teammate-message>

content.text: All five polish tasks are done. Let me do a quick final design review by pulling the latest code and scanning the key files.

content.text: All five commits are in place. Good work from the whole team. Ready for shutdown when the team lead sends the request.

input.content:
Great session. All five polish tasks landed cleanly. From a design perspective, the app now has a solid visual foundation:

- Design tokens are consistent throughout
- Auth forms, todo list, and edit flows all have proper component styling
- HTMX enhancements add interactivity without breaking the visual baseline
- Password toggle and loading indicators follow the design system

The minor CSS gaps I flagged earlier (todo-add__error styling, nav-logout classes, auth-form__message empty state, auth-form__cancel) are low-priority polish items for a future session -- they don't affect functionality or a11y, just visual refinement.

Ready for shutdown.
input.summary: Final design review done, ready for shutdown

routing.summary: Final design review done, ready for shutdown
